summary.verdict: Messaging infrastructure is architecturally sound with centralized EmailSendLog dedup, queued notifications, and feature-gated scheduling. Three critical issues require immediate attention: OnboardingReminder missing email verification gate, WinBack using fragile notifications-table dedup instead of EmailSendLog, and 6 lifecycle commands lacking test coverage.

lifecycle_commands.dedup_note: NON-STANDARD: Uses notifications table query instead of EmailSendLog. Fragile if notifications are pruned.
lifecycle_commands.idempotent_note: Notification-based dedup is fragile; could duplicate if notifications pruned by prune-read-notifications command

lifecycle_commands.schedule_note: Removed to prevent double-sends. SendTrialNudges owns all trial windows.
lifecycle_commands.dedup_note: NON-STANDARD: Uses UserSetting trial_reminder_sent_at with isToday() check instead of EmailSendLog

scoring_commands.purpose: Compute health_score, engagement_score for all users
Lead scoring and MQL/SQL qualification events

monitoring_commands.purpose: Find failed payments, send reminders at 1h/12h

pruning_commands.warning: Pruning read notifications breaks WinBack dedup which queries notifications table

subjects: Features you might not have found yet

lifecycle_notifications.channels_note: CRITICAL BUG: Only mail channel, no database fallback, no hasVerifiedEmail check

lifecycle_notifications.variant_note: Score-based branching: high-intent (>=60) vs standard, paid vs free
issues: Email 3 UTM campaign param says 'reengagement_winback' instead of 'reengagement'
Hardcoded changelog text in email 3 body

lifecycle_notifications.should_queue_note: Should implement ShouldQueue to avoid blocking scheduler

methods.record: Static insert with UniqueConstraintViolationException catch, returns false on duplicate

non_standard_mechanisms.risk: HIGH — notifications pruned after 30 days by prune-read-notifications
MEDIUM — allows re-send on different day, not email-number-based
non_standard_mechanisms.recommendation: Command removed from schedule, so risk is contained. Migrate if re-enabled.

opt_out_system.granularity_note: Single toggle disables all marketing sequences; no per-campaign opt-out

findings.title: OnboardingReminderNotification missing hasVerifiedEmail check and database channel
findings.description: via() returns ['mail'] only. Does not check hasVerifiedEmail() before adding mail channel. No database channel fallback. If user has unverified email, notification is silently lost.
findings.impact: Onboarding emails may be sent to unverified addresses or lost entirely
findings.fix: Add database channel and hasVerifiedEmail() gate matching pattern in all other lifecycle notifications

findings.title: WinBack command uses notifications table for dedup instead of EmailSendLog
findings.description: SendWinBackEmails.alreadySentEmail() queries the notifications table for WinBackNotification with matching email_number, while all other commands use EmailSendLog. The prune-read-notifications command deletes read notifications after 30 days, which would destroy the dedup state and allow duplicate sends.
findings.impact: Win-back emails may be re-sent to users who already received them if notifications are pruned
findings.fix: Migrate to EmailSendLog::alreadySent() and EmailSendLog::record() pattern

findings.title: 6 lifecycle commands have zero test coverage
findings.description: These commands handle email dedup, scoring, and data pruning with no automated verification. Regressions in dedup logic, recipient queries, or opt-out checks would go undetected.
findings.impact: Bugs in untested commands could cause duplicate emails, missed sends, or incorrect scoring

findings.title: SendTrialEndingReminders uses non-standard UserSetting dedup
findings.description: Uses UserSetting 'trial_reminder_sent_at' with isToday() check instead of EmailSendLog. This allows re-sends on subsequent days and is not email-number-based. Risk is contained since command is removed from schedule, but code remains in codebase.
findings.impact: Low (command not scheduled), but could cause issues if re-enabled
findings.fix: Either delete the command or migrate to EmailSendLog if re-enabling

findings.title: Notification pruning conflicts with WinBack dedup mechanism
findings.description: prune-read-notifications deletes read notifications after 30 days. SendWinBackEmails queries the notifications table for dedup. Win-back email 3 is sent at day 30 post-cancellation — exactly when earlier dedup records may be pruned. Race condition between pruning and dedup check.
findings.impact: Win-back email 1 or 2 could be re-sent after notification pruning
findings.fix: Migrate WinBack to EmailSendLog (resolves both MSG-002 and MSG-005)

findings.title: ReEngagement email 3 has wrong UTM campaign parameter
findings.description: Email 3 uses campaign parameter 'reengagement_winback' instead of 'reengagement'. This is a copy-paste error that corrupts funnel analytics attribution.
findings.impact: Re-engagement email 3 traffic misattributed to win-back campaign in GA4

findings.title: EmailSendLog model has no unit tests
findings.description: No tests for EmailSendLog::alreadySent() or EmailSendLog::record() methods. The record() method handles UniqueConstraintViolationException for race conditions — this critical dedup safety net is untested.
findings.impact: Regressions in the dedup system's core model would go undetected

findings.title: LifecycleService has no tests
findings.description: No test file exists for LifecycleService. This service handles stage transitions, audit logging, cache invalidation, and triggers win-back notifications on churn. All side effects are untested.
findings.impact: Stage transition bugs, missing audit logs, or failed win-back triggers would go undetected

findings.title: LeadScoringService has no tests
findings.description: No test file exists for LeadScoringService. The QualifyLeads command depends on this service to compute scores that trigger MQL/SQL qualification events.
findings.impact: Incorrect lead scores could trigger false qualification events or miss legitimate leads

findings.title: ReEngagement email 3 has hardcoded changelog text
findings.description: Email 3 body includes hardcoded text: 'smarter activity tracking and faster load times across the board'. This will become stale and inaccurate over time.
findings.impact: Users receive outdated 'what's new' messaging
findings.fix: Move to config value or query actual changelog

findings.title: AdminHealthAlertNotification is not queued
findings.description: Does not implement ShouldQueue. Health alerts run every 15 minutes via scheduler. Synchronous email sending blocks the scheduler thread during SMTP handshake.
findings.impact: Scheduler delay if mail server is slow; could cascade to delay other scheduled commands

findings.title: No per-campaign unsubscribe granularity
findings.description: Single 'marketing_emails' UserSetting toggle controls all lifecycle sequences. Users cannot opt out of re-engagement emails while keeping onboarding emails.
findings.impact: Users who unsubscribe from one sequence lose all lifecycle communications

findings.title: UnsubscribeController has no HTTP endpoint test
findings.description: UnsubscribeLinkTest.php tests that the HasUnsubscribeLink trait generates links, but no test exercises the actual /unsubscribe/{userId} HTTP endpoint to verify signed URL validation, UserSetting mutation, and response rendering.
findings.impact: Broken unsubscribe flow would not be detected by tests

findings.title: EngagementScoringService has partial test coverage
findings.description: Tests exist but only cover onboarding bonus logic. Login recency scoring (0-25), feature adoption scoring (0-25), and account maturity scoring (0-25) are untested.
findings.impact: Scoring regressions in 3 of 4 dimensions would go undetected

findings.title: CustomerHealthService has partial test coverage
findings.description: Tests exist but only cover basic scoring, email verification, password set, and activation rate. Login frequency, billing status, and retention metrics (d7/d30) are untested.
findings.impact: Health score regressions and retention metric bugs would go undetected

findings.title: Re-engagement hasReengaged() allows unnecessary sends when prior email not logged
findings.description: hasReengaged() queries EmailSendLog for prior email sent_at and compares to last_active_at. If prior email log entry is not found (e.g., first re-engagement email skipped due to opt-out), method returns false and allows the current email to send.
findings.impact: Users may receive email 2/3/4 without having received email 1, creating a confusing sequence

findings.title: No EmailSendLog pruning command exists
findings.description: email_send_logs table grows indefinitely. All other log tables have pruning commands (audit:prune, prune-read-notifications, webhooks:prune-stale). EmailSendLog rows are needed for dedup but old entries (>1 year) are no longer actionable.
findings.impact: Unbounded table growth in production
findings.fix: Add email-send-logs:prune command with safe retention period (e.g., 365 days)

findings.title: DunningReminder plan name fallback is silent
findings.description: resolvePlanName() iterates config('plans.tiers') to find matching stripe_price. If not found, falls back to 'your plan' string with no logging. Missed plan names indicate config/Stripe drift.
findings.impact: Dunning emails may show generic 'your plan' instead of actual plan name without ops visibility
findings.fix: Add Log::warning() when plan name resolution falls back to default

findings.title: ReEngagement toArray() missing userScore and variant type
findings.description: toArray() for database channel does not store userScore or campaignVariant. In-app notification display cannot reconstruct the behavioral branching logic or show score-appropriate content.
findings.impact: In-app notification display may not match email content

findings.title: Welcome-to-onboarding cross-sequence dedup may cause gaps
findings.description: Onboarding email 1 is skipped if welcome_sequence email 2 was already sent. This prevents overlap but means users who received welcome email 2 never get explicit onboarding guidance.
findings.impact: Users who received welcome email 2 may miss onboarding-specific instructions

findings.title: AdminHealthAlert not personalized
findings.description: Uses hardcoded 'Health Check Alert' greeting instead of $notifiable->name. All other notifications personalize greetings.

findings.title: SendTrialEndingReminders command still exists despite being removed from schedule
findings.description: Command was removed from routes/console.php to prevent double-sends with SendTrialNudges, but the command class, notification class, and test file remain. Dead code in codebase.
findings.fix: Delete command, notification, and test if permanently superseded by SendTrialNudges

findings.title: WinBack email 3 at day 30 may race with notification pruning at day 30
findings.description: Win-back email 3 targets subscriptions canceled 30-33 days ago. prune-read-notifications runs daily with 30-day retention. If win-back email 1 was sent and read at day 3, its notification is pruned at day 33 — exactly in the win-back email 3 window.
findings.impact: Timing-dependent duplicate potential for win-back email 1 after pruning
findings.note: Resolved if MSG-002 fix (migrate to EmailSendLog) is applied

findings.title: UTM capture is first-touch only with no last-touch attribution
findings.description: UTM parameters are captured only on first visit (session-based first-touch). No last-touch or multi-touch attribution model available for conversion analysis.
findings.impact: Cannot attribute conversions to most recent marketing touchpoint

findings.title: Welcome email 1 sent via event listener, emails 2-3 via scheduled command
findings.description: WelcomeSequenceNotification email 1 is dispatched synchronously by SendWelcomeNotification listener on Registered event. Emails 2-3 are sent by the scheduled SendWelcomeSequence command. This split makes the sequence harder to reason about and test as a unit.
findings.impact: Maintenance complexity; edge case if listener fails but command succeeds

findings.title: Re-engagement emails run weekly but other sequences run daily
findings.description: SendReEngagementEmails runs weekly (Mondays 09:00) while all other lifecycle commands run daily. Users who become inactive on Tuesday won't receive their 7-day re-engagement email until the following Monday (up to 13 days delay).
findings.impact: Re-engagement timing is imprecise, reducing effectiveness

findings.title: No retry logic in lifecycle commands for individual notification failures
findings.description: All lifecycle commands have try/catch blocks that log errors and continue, but failed individual sends are not retried. The notification itself is queued (ShouldQueue), so queue retry handles transport failures, but if the EmailSendLog::record() call fails after notify(), the notification may be sent but not logged.
findings.impact: Edge case: notification sent but dedup not recorded could cause re-send on next command run

findings.title: AnalyticsEvent enum has 95+ events but no event documentation
findings.description: 95+ analytics events are defined but there is no documentation mapping events to their triggers, expected frequency, or downstream dashboards. Shared events sync test exists but no semantic validation.
findings.impact: Difficult for analytics consumers to understand event taxonomy

findings.title: Signed unsubscribe URLs have no expiration
findings.description: URL::signedRoute() without expiration parameter creates permanent signed URLs. Old emails from months/years ago will still have working unsubscribe links, which could be a security consideration if user IDs are sequential.
findings.impact: Low security risk — unsubscribe is a benign action, but permanent URLs are a mild information leak

findings.title: No integration test for complete email sequence lifecycle
findings.description: Individual command tests exist but no test verifies a complete user journey through sequences (registration → welcome → onboarding → trial nudge → re-engagement → dunning → win-back). Cross-sequence dedup logic is untested at the integration level.
findings.impact: Cross-sequence edge cases (overlapping windows, dedup interactions) may have bugs

findings.title: Email infrastructure uses notification system exclusively (no Mailable classes)
findings.description: All email sending is done via Notification classes with toMail() method. No Mail/Mailable classes exist. This is a valid architectural choice but means all emails must go through the notification pipeline.

findings.title: All lifecycle notifications use both database and mail channels (except OnboardingReminder)
findings.description: Standard pattern: database channel always included, mail channel gated by hasVerifiedEmail(). OnboardingReminderNotification is the exception (MSG-001).

findings.title: LifecycleService auto-queues WinBackNotification on churn transition with 24h delay
findings.description: When a user transitions to CHURNED stage, WinBackNotification is queued with 24-hour delay. This is in addition to the scheduled SendWinBackEmails command. Potential for double-send if both paths trigger.

findings.title: Analytics pipeline has PII sanitization with 34-key blocklist
findings.description: AnalyticsGateway strips 34 PII keys before sending to GA4 Measurement Protocol. Comprehensive and well-tested.

findings.title: Schedule timing creates logical sequence: scoring → qualification → emails
findings.description: 02:00 compute-scores → 06:00 check-expired → 07:00 qualify-leads → 09:00 welcome/re-engagement → 10:00 trial-nudges → 10:30 win-back → 11:00 onboarding. Scoring feeds into qualification which feeds into email targeting.

findings.title: Analytics thresholds configured but only partially wired
findings.description: Defines churn_rate, mrr_drop_percent, trial_conversion thresholds. AdminHealthAlertCommand references health thresholds but not all analytics-thresholds keys are wired to alerts.

findings.title: LifecycleStage enum defines 7 stages with clear funnel ordering
findings.description: VISITOR → TRIAL → ACTIVATED → PAYING → EXPANSION with AT_RISK → CHURNED as churn path. Well-structured lifecycle model.

findings.title: Engagement and health scoring services are batch-optimized
findings.description: Both EngagementScoringService.scoreBatch() and CustomerHealthService.primeHealthScoreCaches() use batch queries to avoid N+1. ComputeUserScores processes in chunks of 200.

recommendations_priority.action: Fix OnboardingReminderNotification via() to add database channel and hasVerifiedEmail check
Migrate SendWinBackEmails to use EmailSendLog instead of notifications table
Add test coverage for untested commands (SendOnboardingReminders, SendDunningReminders) and services (LifecycleService, LeadScoringService, EmailSendLog)
Fix ReEngagement UTM campaign param and hardcoded changelog text
Add UnsubscribeController HTTP test and expand scoring service test coverage
Create EmailSendLog pruning command with 365-day retention
Delete or deprecate SendTrialEndingReminders and TrialEndingNotification if permanently superseded
